note: Issue #283, Fase A: 'measure current coverage, create a real baseline, prevent any drop, require 90% on new/changed code.' The numbers below are REAL, measured by scripts/coverage_gate.py, not invented -- but they are a DEFINED SUBSET baseline, not a full-repo one. See 'scope_rationale'.
scope_rationale: This is the same 21-file set scripts/test_categories.py formalizes as the tests/*_unit.py|_integration.py|_system.py|_regression.py convention -- the only mechanically-categorized subset in the repo today. The full tests/ tree (~1700 tests) was NOT used to measure this baseline: in this repo's sandboxed CI-equivalent shell, an instrumented full-suite run intermittently hangs past a reasonable local-gate budget (some live/e2e tests block on external state) and a handful of subprocess-spawning tests intermittently hit a Windows-sandbox-specific 'WinError 6: invalid handle' OSError unrelated to coverage. Both are pre-existing environment limitations, not introduced by this measurement. Because this baseline only exercises 21 of ~190 test files, the true full-repo percentage is almost certainly higher than the numbers recorded here -- this file intentionally UNDER-claims rather than over-claims coverage.
bug_fixed_to_make_this_measurable: scripts/coverage_gate.py's critical-path percentage previously called the PRIVATE Coverage._analyze(Coverage._get_file_reporter(...)) API pair, which crashes unconditionally on coverage.py 7.15.x + Python 3.14 (PythonFileReporter became unhashable, breaking _analyze's internal dict-keyed cache with TypeError) -- the gate never produced a percentage at all before this PR. Fixed by switching to the public Coverage.analysis2() API.

A.description: Measure current coverage, create a real (if narrow) baseline, never regress it silently. This file is that baseline. scripts/coverage_gate.py already fails closed if a future measurement (over the same --tests-path scope) drops below these numbers, once wired with a --min-baseline check (see 'not_yet_wired' below).

B.description: Raise coverage module-by-module: widen tests_scope in this file (and the corresponding tests/*_<category>.py convention) to cover more of the ~165 currently-uncategorized test files, add tests for modules with clear gaps, remove any temporary exclusions.

C.description: Activate fail_under=85 globally. Blocked on a fast, reliable FULL-suite coverage run existing at all in this environment -- see scope_rationale. Once CI (or a faster local box) can run the whole tests/ tree under coverage in a reasonable time without the WinError 6 / live-test-hang issues, re-measure over tests/ with no --tests-path restriction and replace this file's scope.

D.description: Evaluate raising the global target above 85% once B/C land.

not_yet_wired: scripts/coverage_gate.py does not yet compare a fresh measurement against this baseline file automatically (no --baseline-file/--min-baseline flag) -- it only compares against the fixed --global-threshold/--critical-threshold CLI flags. Wiring an automatic regression check against this file is Fase B/C follow-up work, not attempted in this PR to avoid conflating 'record a real number' with 'build a new enforcement mechanism' in one change.
